text.title: Laravel Layered Patterns (Controller → Service → Resource) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for layered Laravel architecture (codebase-wide).

rule.statement: Each controller method MUST be ≤20 lines AND MUST NOT contain business logic, transactions, or Eloquent queries. Delegate to a service.

rationale.source: Laravel reference — controllers; PSR best practices

rule.rationale: Fat controllers conflate HTTP concerns + business + DB; impossible to test in isolation; hard to reason about.

rule.statement: Every controller action accepting a request body MUST type-hint a Form Request (extending FormRequest). Inline `$request->validate(...)` is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Form Requests separate validation + auth check; isolated tests; localizable messages.

rule.statement: Every JSON response MUST be a JsonResource or Resource Collection. Direct `return $model;` / `response()->json($model->toArray())` is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Resources control which fields leak to clients; version-safe schema evolution.

rule.statement: Multi-write operations MUST live in a service and be wrapped in `DB::transaction`. Controllers MUST NOT call `DB::transaction`.
rule.rationale: Service is the unit-of-work boundary; controller-level tx mixes HTTP + DB scopes.

rule.statement: Resource-level authorization MUST use a Policy class (registered in AuthServiceProvider). Inline `if ($user->id !== $order->user_id) abort(403)` checks are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Centralized authorization is testable + reusable across web/API/console.
